name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: At 4.34% (-2.2%) — first meaningful yield decline in weeks. 'End war' headlines drove some flight-to-safety unwind. But yields dropping while oil stays elevated is UNUSUAL — signals market shifting from 'inflation' to 'growth/recession' concerns. Stagflation trap: yields can't go much higher (growth risk) or lower (inflation risk). Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved. India 10Y GOI at 7.00% (+7bps).

signals.content: 10Y yield topped 4.06% as surging oil prices raised inflation concerns. Typical safe-haven bond rally did NOT materialize.
Yield rose to 4.37%, highest since July 2025. Market removed every rate cut, pricing in possible hike. 'Pandemonium' in global bond markets.

US 10Y at 4.44% (+10.5% monthly). Oil-driven inflation expectations rising (~40bps in March). Fed trapped in stagflation — can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). 2Y at 3.61% = 83bps positive slope, but both elevated = stagflation pricing. India 10Y at 6.93% (+6bps) reflecting parallel pressure. ECB froze rate cuts March 19. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) on hold simultaneously — global monetary policy paralysis.

US 10Y dropped to 4.34% (-2.2%) — first meaningful yield decline in weeks. 'End war' headlines drove some flight-to-safety unwind. But yields dropping while oil stays elevated is UNUSUAL — signals market shifting from 'inflation' to 'growth/recession' concerns. Stagflation trap: yields can't go much higher (growth risk) or lower (inflation risk). Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved — curve dynamics. India 10Y GOI at 7.00% (+7bps). CONFIRMED.

edges.context: 10Y dropped to 4.34% — yields dropping while oil elevated signals shift from inflation to growth/recession concerns. Market pricing 52% hike probability. Stagflation trap visible in curve dynamics.
Yield decline to 4.34% modest relief but 2Y barely moved — curve dynamics unclear. S&P at 6,343.72. VIX below 30 on peace headlines.
Higher US yields → capital flows to US → away from EM including India. Contributes to FII outflows.

trigger_points.mechanism: 5% was the level that crashed markets in Oct 2023. Would signal inflation expectations unanchored. Massive equity selloff risk.
